setup_task.sh PaymentProcessorTest.java (lines 188-257) contains exactly 8 @Test methods: testCreditCardFee, testDebitCardFee, testBankTransferFee, testDigitalWalletFee, testInvalidAmount, testBankTransferLimit, testInvalidTypeLogic, testLabelGeneration

privileged_info_summary: The task is to refactor a legacy Java Maven project that uses integer magic constants for payment types (0=Credit Card, 1=Debit Card, 2=Bank Transfer, 3=Digital Wallet) into a type-safe enum. The project at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/payment-processor must have a new enum PaymentType created in package com.payments.model with four constants (CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, BANK_TRANSFER, DIGITAL_WALLET) and two abstract methods: getFeeRate() with rates [0.029, 0.015, 0.005, 0.025] respectively, and getDisplayName() with labels ['Credit Card', 'Debit Card', 'Bank Transfer', 'Digital Wallet']. Four production classes (PaymentProcessor, FeeCalculator, PaymentValidator, PaymentReport) must be refactored to use the enum instead of int constants, the legacy PaymentConstants class must be removed, and all 8 unit tests in PaymentProcessorTest must pass with the project compiling successfully.
